rationale: Add a small procedural config note to bias future steps toward file-based payloads, reducing Windows CLI arg-length risk without touching code.

edits.content:
{
  "schema": "brainflow.procedural.v1",
  "name": "cli_arg_safety",
  "goal": "Reduce Windows CLI argument-length risk by preferring file-based payloads.",
  "rules": [
    "Prefer writing large LLM prompts or payloads to temp files and pass file paths instead of inline CLI args.",
    "Avoid embedding long JSON in command arguments; use @file or stdin where supported.",
    "Keep per-command arguments short; split work into multiple steps if needed."
  ],
  "scope": "global"
}

risks: May be ignored by code paths that do not consult procedural memory.
expected_benefit: Guides future workflow steps toward safer, shorter CLI calls, reducing arg-length failures on Windows.
